title: Update working memory from issue 350 auth session
summary: Durable context from the session that changed remote auth setup handling, rejected remote first-user auth setup, and added Entire/Biome repository workflow facts.

claims.text: When no credentials are configured and check_auth returns SetupRequired, auth_gate keeps API and WebSocket requests at 401 AUTH_SETUP_REQUIRED, allows local page requests through with a loopback identity, and redirects non-local page requests to /setup-required instead of letting them reach the SPA.
The web UI registers /setup-required as a page route; setup_required_handler renders SpaTemplate::SetupRequired through the normal SPA template renderer.

The session rejected the earlier auth-only onboarding approach for already-onboarded instances, including an auth_setup_pending Gon flag and onboarding-view authOnly mode, because it could let the first remote user on a Tailscale or bind deployment claim the instance.

After onboarding is complete but authentication is not configured, users should configure auth from localhost or the CLI; remote users should see access-denied/setup-required guidance rather than a password or passkey setup form.

The repository tracks Entire project config in .entire/settings.json with enabled true, telemetry false, and session summarization enabled; .entire/.gitignore excludes local tmp, metadata, logs, and settings.local.json content.
The session decided not to add .entire/ to the root .gitignore; Entire local subpaths should be ignored by .entire/.gitignore so shared settings can still be committed.
Biome is configured against schema 2.4.6, keeps noUnusedExpressions under linter.rules.suspicious, and formats JavaScript with tab indentation.

sources.title: SWE-chat Claude Code session 6ce656a2 issue 350 investigation

metadata.reason: The user identified the security risk that a first remote visitor could set auth, and the assistant explicitly reverted the auth-only onboarding plan in favor of access denied guidance.
The user requested access denied for remote already-onboarded instances and said auth setup should happen via terminal CLI or localhost.
The session checked Entire documentation and concluded that shared settings should be committed while local metadata is ignored by the nested .entire/.gitignore.
